summary: Phase 1 Foundation + Core MVP: CLI Adapter, Delegate Executor, server API routes, frontend components, and test coverage for WSGateway/StateSyncEngine
approach: Build the missing CLI integration layer (CLIAdapter + DelegateExecutor) first as it is the biggest gap and blocks real data flow. In parallel, add test coverage for the already-implemented WSGateway and StateSyncEngine. Then wire server API routes and integration in index.ts. Finally, build the two frontend panels (Project Radar tree + Workflow Commander cards) that consume the data. This follows the data-flow direction: CLI subprocess -> EventBus -> WSGateway -> frontend.

waves.label: CLI Adapter + WSGateway/StateSync tests (parallel, no file conflicts)
Delegate Executor (depends on TASK-001 for DelegateEvent type in shared/types.ts)
Frontend components — Project Radar + Workflow Commander (parallel, no file conflicts)
Server API routes + index.ts wiring + +page.svelte integration (depends on all prior tasks)

data_flow.diagram:
maestro CLI --(NDJSON stdout)--> CLIAdapter --(WorkflowMeta[])--> Hono API
maestro delegate --(broker events)--> DelegateExecutor --(DelegateEvent)--> EventBus
EventBus --(InternalEvent)--> WSGateway --(WSMessage)--> WSClient
chokidar --(fs events)--> StateSyncEngine --(StateSyncEvent)--> EventBus
Hono API --(HTTP JSON)--> Svelte frontend
WSClient --(store updates)--> ProjectRadar + WorkflowCommander

design_decisions.rationale: User decision locked. Run `maestro --version`, parse major version, select matching adapter from registry. Fail-fast with UnsupportedVersionError if no match.

design_decisions.choice: Spawn + line-by-line stdout parsing + EventBus emission
design_decisions.rationale: Delegate broker emits NDJSON events (queued/started/completed/failed). Parse each line, emit to EventBus, let WSGateway push to clients. Process cleanup on stop/exit.

design_decisions.choice: Self-built Svelte tree + card components (no external tree library)
design_decisions.rationale: MVP scope; tree depth is fixed (milestone->phase->step, 3 levels). No need for virtualization or external dependency. Keeps bundle small.

design_decisions.choice: Unit tests with mocked dependencies (mock WebSocket, mock chokidar, real EventBus)
design_decisions.rationate: Follow existing vitest pattern from event-bus.test.ts. Mock external I/O, test internal logic. Target >80% coverage.

patterns: EventBus pub/sub (subscribe returns unsubscribe fn, publish returns InternalEvent)
Constructor injection (EventBus, TranslatorMiddleware passed to WSGateway/StateSyncEngine)
Singleton exports (eventBus in event-bus.ts, wsClient in stores/index.ts)

pressure_pass.notes: DelegateExecutor is highest-complexity task (subprocess lifecycle + output parsing + EventBus integration). read_first covers all dependencies. convergence.criteria are grep-verifiable. action is concrete with exact method signatures and spawn arguments.

residual_risks: CLI output format assumed from maestro-flow source code — not tested against live CLI
WS push latency < 500ms not measured in real network conditions (mock timers only)
